summary:
The test evaluates changes in an individual's mass occurring shortly after death by measuring variations due to decomposition processes. It is not concerned with air presence within body cavities like the ears immediately post-death but instead looks at long-term bodily transformations indicative of mortality status several hours later.
To refine this summary for clarity while maintaining its essence, it should focus more succinctly on critical aspects without extraneous details such as decompositions specifically taking place "several hours" afterward which was inferred unnecessarily from context clues provided initially.
The revised version would be clearer if stated simply thus:
Ploucquet's test assesses subtle shifts in an individual's mass right after death through measurement techniques sensitive enough to capture early signs of decay; these tests do not involve checking for trapped gases near vital organs upon initial inspection.
This revision maintains accuracy whilst condensing into two concise statements focused strictly around core elements pertinent to understanding what makes up effective assessment using Ploucquet's It is not concerned with air presence within body cavities like the ears immediately post-death but instead looks at long-term bodily transformations indicative of mortality status several hours later.
